steps.title: Identify the key acknowledgment
steps.content: Text 2 paleontologists state “the skulls looked avian at first” while explaining that detailed studies revealed these were actually unusual lizards. This acknowledges that the initial observation of avian-like features was valid, explaining why the researchers' initial thought made sense.

steps.title: Apply to the researchers' initial thought
steps.content:
Choice A expresses exactly what Text 2 paleontologists would conclude: the initial thought is understandable because the fossil features do look avian, yet O. khaungraae is probably a lizard based on detailed evidence.

The other three either mischaracterize what confused the researchers, wrongly attribute their assumptions, or introduce unsupported details.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
